title: Synthetic World Scenario
description: Defines a reproducible world configuration for the headless runner. Each scenario pins a world geometry, agent count, and simulation parameters so runs are deterministic given a seed.

name.description: Unique identifier for the scenario. Used as the preset name in --preset <name> and in output file naming.

description.description: Human-readable summary of what this scenario tests or demonstrates.

seed.description: Default PRNG seed. CLI --seed overrides this value. Controls agent placement, food spawns, and all stochastic decisions.

steps.description: Default number of simulation ticks. CLI --steps overrides this value.

world.description: World geometry and initial agent count.

size.description: Side length of the square torus grid. Total cells = size^2. Larger worlds spread agents thinner (density = initial_population / size^2).

initial_population.description: Number of agents spawned at tick 0. Each starts at a random grid cell with energy = 1.0.

params.description: Simulation knobs that control agent behavior and environment dynamics.

energy_decay.description: Energy lost per tick per agent, regardless of movement. Higher values create harsher survival pressure. Range [0, 1]: 0.005 = gentle, 0.01 = moderate, 0.05 = brutal.

move_speed.description: Maximum cells an agent can move per tick (Manhattan distance). 0 = stationary, 1 = normal, >1 = fast scouts.

food_spawn_rate.description: Probability per tick that a new food item spawns at a random empty cell. Controls resource abundance. Range [0, 1]: 0.02 = scarce, 0.1 = moderate, 0.5 = abundant.

food_energy.description: Energy gained when an agent consumes a food item. Higher values make each food encounter more impactful.
